tldr: This quarter, the cohort rotated capital away from select mega-cap tech and consumer discretionary names, opting to reduce exposure in heavily owned consensus trades. Proceeds were aggressively redeployed into semiconductor infrastructure, wide-moat financial data providers, and payment networks, signaling a preference for structural AI hardware plays and defensive, high-margin compounders.

themes.heading: Accumulating Semiconductor & AI Infrastructure
themes.detail: Managers aggressively added to semiconductor foundries, broad industry ETFs, and chipmakers. With 4 managers buying TSM and 3 initiating/buying the SMH ETF, the cohort is clearly favoring the 'picks-and-shovels' hardware layer of the AI trade.

themes.heading: Pivoting to Wide-Moat Financials & Data Monopolies
themes.detail: There was strong consensus buying in payment networks and credit rating/data providers. Visa saw 5 buyers (including 3 initiations), while S&P Global and Moody's also saw multiple adds, reflecting a defensive tilt toward high-margin compounders with pricing power.

themes.heading: Trimming Mega-Cap Tech & Consumer Discretionary
themes.detail: Investors took profits or reduced exposure in heavily owned mega-cap tech names, with MSFT and AMZN seeing 5 and 4 sellers respectively. The group also leaned out of consumer-facing retail, travel, and dining stocks.

themes.heading: Niche Initiations in Media and Industrials
themes.detail: A notable cluster of managers initiated new positions in specific media and industrial/equipment rental names, hunting for value outside of crowded tech trades. VSNT was the most-initiated stock overall with 4 new buyers.
